Acceptance Criterion: Operator CRDs are installed and available through the Kubernetes API.
Operator deployment is created in the target namespace.
Operator pod reaches Running and Ready state.
Operator status reports Success / Installed.
Helm install succeeds with default values.
Helm upgrade preserves existing configuration and running workloads.
Operator watches only its own namespace.
Operator reconciles Neo4j CRs created in its namespace.
Operator does not reconcile Neo4j CRs created in another namespace.
RBAC is namespace-scoped and does not require cluster-wide permissions except CRD access.
Operator watches only the configured namespaces.
Operator reconciles Neo4j CRs in each configured namespace.
Operator ignores non-configured namespaces.
RBAC grants access only to configured namespaces.
Operator watches Neo4j CRs across all namespaces.
Operator reconciles Neo4j CRs across the cluster.
ClusterRole and ClusterRoleBinding are created and validated.
Operator creates, updates, and deletes child resources to match the CR desired state.
Manual drift on managed resources is detected and corrected.
CR status exposes Ready, Reconciling, Error, and reason/message fields.
Errors are surfaced in CR conditions without requiring log inspection.
CR status exposes operation phase for install, upgrade, backup, restore, and maintenance.
Operator upgrade completes without deleting managed Neo4j workloads.
Existing CRs continue to reconcile after operator upgrade.
CRD version conversion preserves existing spec and status fields.
Operator components are deleted.
Managed Neo4j data is preserved by default.
RBAC permissions match selected operator scope.
Operator emits logs for reconciliation start, success, and failure.
Operator exposes metrics for reconcile count, failures, and duration.
Neo4j workload resources are created from the CR.
Neo4j pod(s) reach Running and Ready state.
Neo4j responds to driver connectivity on Bolt.
CR status reports Neo4j deployment as Ready.
Exactly one Neo4j server is deployed.
Standalone Neo4j accepts authenticated read/write queries.
Expected number of Neo4j members are created.
Cluster forms and reaches expected minimum cluster size.
Cluster roles and routing are validated through the Neo4j driver.
Enterprise deployment requires explicit license acceptance or evaluation mode.
Deployment fails with a clear error if Enterprise license is missing.
Neo4j configuration values from the CR are rendered into the workload.
Invalid configuration is rejected or surfaced with a clear status error.
APOC configuration values are applied to Neo4j.
APOC credential secrets are mounted at the configured path.
Neo4j initial password is generated or read from the configured Secret.
Secret mounts are available inside the Neo4j pod at the configured path.
LDAP password secret is mounted and referenced by Neo4j configuration.
Neo4j image registry, repository, tag, and pull policy are applied.
Image pull secrets are referenced by the Neo4j pod.
TLS secrets are mounted for configured connectors.
Bolt and HTTPS endpoints use the configured TLS policy.
Cluster communication uses configured TLS when enabled.
TLS certificate reload is enabled when configured.
Data PVC is created or bound according to the selected storage variant.
Neo4j data persists across pod restart.
PVC uses the default StorageClass when default storage is selected.
PVC uses the named StorageClass when dynamic storage is selected.
Neo4j binds to the configured existing PVC.
Neo4j binds to a volume matching the configured selector.
PVC is created from the configured volumeClaimTemplate.
Cloud object storage access uses platform workload identity (e.g. GKE Workload Identity or AKS Workload Identity).
Cloud object storage access uses mounted credentials or Kubernetes Secrets without workload identity.
Service is created with the selected service type and targets Neo4j pods.
Only the configured connectors (HTTP, HTTPS, Bolt) are exposed; disabled connectors are not published on the service.
Service ports match the configured connector port numbers.
ClusterIP service is created and reachable only from within the cluster.
Service is not assigned an external IP or NodePort.
LoadBalancer service is created when requested.
External IP or hostname is assigned and routes traffic to Neo4j on configured ports.
NodePort service is created when requested.
Each enabled connector is reachable via a stable node IP and assigned NodePort.
Bolt port is exposed on the service and accepts authenticated driver connections.
HTTP and HTTPS ports are not exposed when Bolt-only profile is selected.
HTTP port is exposed and Neo4j Browser responds over HTTP.
HTTPS port is exposed and Neo4j Browser responds over HTTPS.
Bolt and HTTP ports are not exposed when HTTPS-only profile is selected.
Both HTTP and Bolt ports are exposed and reachable.
HTTPS port is not exposed when HTTP + Bolt profile is selected.
Both HTTPS and Bolt ports are exposed and reachable.
HTTP port is not exposed when HTTPS + Bolt profile is selected.
HTTP, HTTPS, and Bolt ports are all exposed on the service.
Each exposed connector accepts connections according to its protocol.
HTTP and HTTPS ports are exposed; Bolt port is not exposed.
Browser access works over both HTTP and HTTPS.
Internal ports/configuration required for multi-cluster communication are enabled.
Node selectors, affinity, tolerations, and topology spread constraints are applied to Neo4j pods.
Termination grace period and service account are applied when configured.
Startup, readiness, and liveness probes are configured on Neo4j pods.
Neo4j is not marked ready before Bolt is available.
Configuration change is detected by the operator.
Required restart or rolling restart is executed safely.
Cluster scale-out creates additional members.
New server is enabled and joins the cluster when required.
Cluster scale-in removes members safely without data loss under supported conditions.
Preflight validation checks cluster health before upgrade.
Neo4j version/image is upgraded successfully.
Neo4j remains available according to the selected deployment mode during upgrade.
Backup storage is created or mounted.
Backup job completes successfully and produces backup artifacts.
User can trigger an on-demand backup.
Scheduled backups run according to the configured schedule.
Cloud credentials are mounted or workload identity is used for backup/restore.
Restore job completes successfully from configured backup source.
Restored Neo4j instance starts and accepts queries.
Neo4j can restore from configured seed URI.
Neo4j metrics endpoint is available when enabled.
ServiceMonitor is created when enabled and CRD exists.
Neo4j logs are written to the configured location.
Custom logging XML is applied when configured.
Offline maintenance mode restarts workload without running Neo4j process.
Maintenance job is created and completes successfully.
Maintenance job result is exposed in CR status.
Neo4j workload resources are deleted.
PVCs are preserved by default.
Data PVCs remain after CR deletion when preservation is selected.
PVCs are deleted only when explicit delete-data policy is selected.
